FT_IC1.evidence: Uses ABCD Study data; n=6,758 individuals from ABCD wave 1 release fMRI data

FT_IC2.evidence: Paper mentions 'more screen time (F = 6.80, p < .0001)' as a non-SM outcome associated with neurodevelopmental profiles. No specific smartphone or social media variable measured. Generic 'screen time' is not qualifying per FT-IC2 exclusion criteria.

FT_IC3.evidence: Screen time reported only as outcome variable of the latent profiles (contingent upon profile membership), not as an independent exposure linked to non-SM variables. No phone/SM → outcome association reported.

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical ABCD analysis; latent profile analysis with cross-validated design and multiple outcomes tested

FT_IC6.evidence: Journal article; peer-reviewed in Biological Psychiatry: Cognitive Neuroscience and Neuroimaging

FT_EC7_reason: FT-EC7 is irrelevant; paper fails FT-IC2 (no specific smartphone/SM exposure). Excluded at FT-IC2 stage.
notes: Paper uses ABCD data and reports fMRI-based neurodevelopmental profiles. Screen time is mentioned only as one outcome variable correlating with profiles, not as a measured exposure variable. Does not meet smartphone/social media specificity requirement.
